title: About
seoDescription: About Jack Wingate — graphic and UI/UX designer, First-Class graduate, transport and product design.

blocks.body:
I'm Jack Wingate, a First-Class Graphic Design graduate from the University of Huddersfield (2025), based in West Yorkshire. I'm a designer first — my work spans brand, UI/UX, and product design, with a particular interest in transport, wayfinding, and realism-led interfaces that feel intuitive rather than abstract.

At university I led large self-initiated projects from research through to delivery, including a national Great British Railways brand and app concept, a 10,000-word dissertation on the lack of realism in modern UI with an interactive outcome, and WEBTEXT — a teletext-inspired digital service. Outside of coursework I've designed and shipped Rail Statistics, a self-initiated iOS and Android app, from Figma prototypes to public release.

I work primarily in Figma and Adobe Creative Suite. When something needs to exist as a real app or website, I use AI-assisted workflows to build from my own designs and specs — keeping full ownership of the visual direction and UX. Whether it's campaign graphics, motion, environmental design, or mobile product work, I care about hierarchy, accessibility, and follow-through.

items.title: BTEC Level 3 National Extended Diploma in Art & Design
items.subtitle: Wakefield College

items.title: Rail Statistics
items.description: End-to-end product and UI/UX design for a self-initiated mobile app helping rail enthusiasts log stations and tickets. Designed skeuomorphic visual identity and interaction patterns, then used AI-assisted builds from my Figma designs to reach public release on the Apple App Store and Google Play.

items.title: Great British Railways — Brand & App Concept
items.description: People-first national rail brand identity with a rigorous system across mobile, print, station signage, and wayfinding. Explored accessible information hierarchy and regional-dialect messaging to balance national consistency with local character.

items.title: UI/UX Dissertation & Interactive Outcome
items.description: 10,000-word research project on the lack of realism in modern UI and its impact on user understanding, paired with an interactive skeuomorphic interface applying tactile, realism-led principles.

items.title: WEBTEXT — Interactive Teletext Service
items.description: Interactive teletext-style service modernising retro-tech aesthetics for contemporary digital use, with navigation and layout patterns balancing nostalgia and accessibility.

items.title: Live industry & charity work
items.description: Public mural for The Ridings shopping centre; typographic zine for CAP Care Charity raising awareness of homelessness; D&AD and Creative Conscience competition entries with team leadership and fast-turnaround deliverables.

items.title: Graduate Showcase
items.description: Presented final-year projects to industry professionals and the public at The Graduate Showcase, Huddersfield.

items: Vibe coding apps & sites from designs

blocks.heading: Interests
items: YouTube — travel, rail, and visual storytelling (filming, editing, publishing)
Exploring cities to study signage, typography, and public-facing design in context
Classic menswear and tailoring — proportion, texture, colour, and presentation
